system: You are an ambitious AI PhD student focused on improving causal structure discovery from observational data using the gCastle framework.
task_description:
You are working with the gCastle framework to discover causal DAG structure from observational data. The task uses synthetic data generated from 50-node Erdos-Renyi random graphs with nonlinear (MLP-based) structural equations and only 500 samples. The baseline is NOTEARS with a linear model assumption, which is intentionally mismatched to the nonlinear data — it will perform poorly. Your goal is to minimize Structural Hamming Distance (SHD) between the predicted and true causal graphs. You may modify the model (e.g., switch to nonlinear NOTEARS-MLP), change the optimization procedure, regularization strategy, thresholding method, acyclicity penalty, or propose entirely new structure learning approaches.

You are evaluated on a validation set during development. Your final performance will be measured on a separate test set that uses the same DAG structure but different noise realizations. The validation and test data are generated independently. Use the validation results to guide your research.

The run_causal_discovery(X, true_dag) function must accept a data matrix X and return a binary adjacency matrix. Do not change this function signature. Do not use the true_dag parameter in your algorithm — it is provided only for debugging.
